This position is located in the Office of Ship Operations, Office of the Deputy Associate Administrator for Federal Sealift, Office of the Associate Administrator for Strategic Sealift, Maritime Administration (MARAD).

The Office of Ship Operations is responsible for (1) setting policy and providing guidance and oversight for Ready Reserve Force (RRF) planning and program execution to support maintenance and repair, activations, deactivations, and dock and sea trials; (2) management of technical programs designed to maximize RRF readiness and maintain state schoolships to support national strategic sealift needs, and preserve and maintain assets; (3) performing the administrative and management oversight of the Maritime Administrationâs Ship Manager Contracts and General Agency Agreements, and (4) providing technical support to other MARAD Offices and Divisions.

This position is established to develop policy and monitor policy compliance relating to the daily management of the Ready Reserve Force (RRF) and National Defense Reserve Fleet (NDRF) program. The work involves performing in-depth analysis of program planning and execution to present information on vessels that are in MARAD custody.

The** ideal candidate**for this position is a senior level individual that must possess knowledge using financial, budget, and accounting systems.

**A Pre-Employment/Pre-appointment drug test is required of applicants selected for hire in positions designated as safety or security sensitive positions. For these positions an offer of employment is contingent upon a negative pre-employment/pre-appointment test results.**

* Serve as the Agencyâs point of contact for POM and budget issues and resource inquiries from program and resource sponsors including U.S. TRANSCOM, OPNAV N42, and Military Sealift Command (MSC).

* Develop all necessary historical information and summary data as a guide for the RMB's financial recommendations.

Description

Randstad US in partnership with Partners Healthcare is hiring a Senior Financial Analyst for a temp-to-perm position.The Analyst's primary responsibility will be the revenue valuation, reporting and budgeting functions for the Mass General Hospital. Working closely with the CFO, budget office, revenue finance managers and staff provide technical expertise for all aspects of NPSR and ensure the net revenue and reserve levels for the financial statements are properly stated. Junior staff will be provided in support of this function. There is opportunity to be part of the Reimbursement Team with the preparation of the Federal (Medicare), State (CHIA) and UCCR cost reports including Area Wage Index /Occupational Mix, DSH, Pass-through Reimbursement. There is significant interaction with internal and external customers.

Working hours: Monday-Friday 8-4:30

Skills:
--Support Net Revenue Manger and work closely with other managers and analysts to ensure that Net Revenue information is accurate and monthly financial close deadlines are met.
--Support Net Revenue Manager with the development of the annual revenue budgets and quarterly forecasts.
--Support Net Revenue Manager in ensuring that all AR reserve balances are properly stated.
--Work with Government Payer Policy, Reimbursement managers and staff to ensure Medicare reimbursement and settlements, as well as other Third Party Settlements are properly recorded.
--Participate in the development of entity cash budgets.
--Perform analysis for the purpose of analyzing actual to budget variances and other data issues that have a bearing on accurate revenue measurement.
--Assist in the development of work processes and documentation.
--Provide analytic support to Partners and Entity CFO as requested.

--Assist the Reimbursement Team with the preparation of the Medicare, CHIA, UCCR Reports and Champus filing as necessary.
--Work with entity and outside consultants to ensure that proper records are available to support the AWI/Occ Mix, DSH, and Medicare Pass-through costs and how this affects NPSR.
--Support the filing of other required governmental filing as may be determined for the Government Revenue department to complete (State Home Office cost report HCF-1 and State SNF cost report HCF-3).

--Bachelor's degree required in Finance and/or Healthcare. Masters degree preferred.
--Five + years of health care finance analytic experience in an academic medical setting, including in-depth knowledge of governmental and private payer methodologies strongly preferred.
--Strong quantitative and analytical skills; solid knowledge of database management systems, Excel and Access.

"Ability to work independently and contribute in a team environment
